turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: submitted We'll hear argument next in number oh oh eight thirty-two, National Cable and Telecommunications Association versus Gulf Power Company. Mr. Feldman. [Inaudible]

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court. Under the Pole Attachments Act, the FCC is required to regulate pole attachments to ensure the the rates, terms, and conditions for those attachments be just and reasonable. The question presented in this case is whether two particular types of c- attachments are covered by the act. The first is an attachment to provide cable television service and commingled Internet access. That means that on, over that particular wire at the particular time is traveling both cable television service and Internet access at different frequencies. The second type of attachment at issue is an attachment used to provide wireless telecommunications services. The FCC determined that, and the most natural reading of the act requires that both types of attachments are covered. The operative provision, the basic coverage provision, defines a pole attachment as any attachment by a cable television system or provider of telecommunications service. [Inaudible]

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Where do we find the text of the act?

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: In the appendix to the, our petition for certiorari right at the end. The language that I'm talking about now is on page two hundred and five A. [Inaudible]

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: it's, I'm sure it's in our brief also, but it's not in app- it's not separately set forth in an appendix there. [Inaudible]

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: But the, in , sect- the section I'm referring to now is section two twenty-four A four. It says, the term pole attachment means, any attachment by a cable television system or provider of telecommunications service to a pole, duct, conduit, or right-of-way owned or controlled by a utility. [Inaudible]

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: And don't you think it's implicit in that definition that it mean, in , not just an attachment by, but also an attachment for the purpose of the business of?

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: They couldn't put up a billboard, you know yo- you know, i-

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: Right. I think that the use of the term, in particular, cable television system, it has to be part of the cable television system, which could be reasonably construed to mean the the network of of of devices that are used to provide cable television system service to people. I think that's correct, and I think probably the same thing is true with the...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: is Inter- Internet access part of a cable television system?

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: The FCC hasn't reached a conclusion on that yet because what the FCC concluded, and I think what the la- most natural reading of the statute leads to the conclusion as well that if an attachment is an attachment that's used to provide cable television system service, the fact, or it's used by a cable television system, the fact that it's also used for something else doesn't exclude...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: If it still is well, it would still be.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: cover- covered under E, under under the telecommunications rate.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: I I wouldn't quite say that.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: it has purported to reserve that question, hasn't it?

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: I I wouldn't quite put it that way.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Where where do we find that?

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: That is on page eighty, the best place to start with is eighty-seven A at paragraph thirty-three.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: you think that that issue is up in this case, then, whether...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: saying that the that the that the decision here rests upon that.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: here is not whether the court of appeals was right, it's whether the commission was wrong.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: rate issue was the the the the court of appeals did not d- did not address, and I don't even think was presented to the court of appeals, what the proper rate is to apply.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Yes, but theoretically it could be...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: as a result of that, we don't know? We cannot tell.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I I don't think that that's correct, what...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: for us to say to the agency, you've got to explain to us what you at least believe your jurisdictional basis is for this because, depending on whether your jurisdictional basis is the general provision or D or E may affect that that that may, the the result in in this case could be dependent on that.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: I think the agency was very clear that it jurisdi-, its basis for jurisdiction is A four, which is the basic coverage provision of the act, it could, it, and it said...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: then that forces u-, in other words, you're saying the agency made it clear that it was neither D nor E.

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: All right, and I agree. The it, You can infer, you're right. You can infer that...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: and they said it could be D because it c-, we're not, we don't have to decide whether it is a t- a-, whether the Internet access part of it is a a provision of, is a a cable service because if it's a cable service, it's covered under the terms of D.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Okay, but if i- if it's...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: if it's under D, we we review it strictly within D terms.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: then we've got to decide another issue.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: But I I think the agency was quite clear, and they pinned their...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: question of then whether it's D or not is, just has to do with the question of what the right rate is.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Mr. Feldman, are you saying that it's an...

turns.name: RUTH GINSBURG
turns.text: academic question what the precise rate is when we're dealing with a court of appeals decision that says that's irrelevant because there is no authority at all...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: think th- that the point is that is not a a, even almost not a, probably not a possible reading of the statute, and certainly not the one that the FCC, once the FCC adopted a...

turns.name: RUTH GINSBURG
turns.text: you're asking us to say that F, the FCC does have authority.

turns.name: RUTH GINSBURG
turns.text: basic question is does it have any authority to come up with a just and reasonable rate at all.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And I I would I would a-

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: also the case that we are being forced to decide an issue which, if the agency were clear and came out and said it's D, we wouldn't have to decide.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: I I don't think that that's right because whet- whether the agency says it's D or not, the fact is that A four covers this.

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: Well, yeah, but that's the, that's one of the...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: realize that, but that iss- that issue, it seems to me, is not a difficult one.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: doing that, which I can go into.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: I can give you another another plausible explanation.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: of why Congress put cable only in the later section and in the earlier section just talked about...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: anything else that cable does will be telecommunications.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: Well, if Congress thought that again, the the result of of if that were true could be that the E rate might apply to this thing, but it would still be that the E rate applied.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: But let's assume that FCC determines that this is not telecommunications.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: And I think they have determined for the time being...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: and you have a a hybrid cable.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: If it turned out that this additional the addition of Internet access meant that it was neither cable nor telecommunications ser- service, that that that additional service was neither...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: neither of those, then the FCC would have to figure out what the just and reasonable rate would be for that.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: more for a hybrid than for a pure cable.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: possible the FCC could do that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: the FCC didn't and why the statute...

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: I'm wondering why that can be because it has no jurisdiction over pure Internet.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: but then I, but my question is why can it possibly charge more for the hybrid...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: I don't want to justify that decision because what the FCC decided is that that wouldn't be what it would want to do, and it, if it it decided that if it's a hybrid like...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: They're they're they're they're saying even though the section says you charge this rate when it's cable only, we're going to say you can charge this rate when it's cable plus Internet.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: Yes, yes because I think that what Congress wanted to specify when it said when it's cable only is it wanted to ensure that, and and this is actually fairly clear, is when the cable companies went into the into the business of providing telephone service to people, that they would ha- be charged the same rate as the telecommunications providers who were newly added to the act.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: If you ignore the the doctrine inclusio unius, exclusio alterius, and you say these are the rates when it's cable...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: And the FCC has come and said, these are also the rates when it's cable...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: If that were true, that could lead to the conclusion that the FCC's choice of rate here was wrong and maybe it should have chosen the E rate or some other rate.

turns.name: RUTH GINSBURG
turns.text: Mr. Feldman, do I understand your argument that if we should reverse the Eleventh Circuit and say there is FCC jurisdiction, then it would go then it would have to await your ongoing rulemaking to determine which category this is, or would there be something for the Eleventh Amendment to do on remand once we say you have jurisdiction to do something?

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: I think that that if the issue was preserved before the Eleventh Circuit, they would have jurisdiction to determine what the right rate is, and they d-, look at what what the FCC's reasoning was and whether, you know, the the various conclusions the FCC reached in coming to the D rate.

turns.name: PETER D. KEISLER
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: the attachment of a cable system.

turns.name: PETER D. KEISLER
turns.text: Well, it's an attachment by a cable television system to the pole, and the system itself is defined as a set of closed transmission paths and associated signal and generation reception control equipment that is designed to provide cable services which include video programming.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: But but but but but but that means that they can get away with it only if they are correct that in in choosing to apply the D rate.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: selection of the D rate is wrong, then their assumption that they don't have to get into that question is also wrong.

turns.name: PETER D. KEISLER
turns.text: It could only be wrong in two circumstances.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: You you say they haven't sought cert on it, but but they're they're the respondents here.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Well, but it seems to me they're they're entitled to defend the the the outcome below on any reaso- on any ground.

turns.name: PETER D. KEISLER
turns.text: But they haven't defended on that ground, Justice Scalia.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: talk for a minute about the attachment of wireless...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Telecommunications, we we don't have a position.

turns.name: PETER D. KEISLER
turns.text: But but the packing of multiple services into this wire, that was considered a good thing.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Internet is concerned, you wouldn't be kicked off.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: What is this conductory citation here?

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: It's fourty-seven USC, section two two four B one.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: And the page reference again? Excuse me.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: It's two oh six A of the appendix to the petition.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: B one says, in pertinent part, that the commission shall regulate the rates, terms, and conditions for pole attachments.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: No, i- it's it's even funnier than that.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: But it includes nailing up a wireless thing.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Well, the government's, on this issue and also on the inter issue, Internet issue, they've they've ignored some of the basic rules of reading a statute, and what they've ignored here is the basic rule that you have to read the whole statute and the meaning that any of the phrases, like any attachment here, is informed by context.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: But before you turn to the next section, staying with A four for a minute...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: any attachment to a pole, duct, conduit, or right-of-way owned or controlled by a utility.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Does that language apply to what we're talking about?

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: literally the definition does apply regardless of the purpose of the attachment.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: If that's if you stop reading if you stop reading the statute there, you could come to that conclusion, but you need to read the whole statute, and if you do, sir, with respect, in D and E there are the two rate formulas.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: may I just cut you short because I want to get m- th- one thought out of my mind.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Do you r- read it as saying provided that the attachment is used either for a D or an E purpose?

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: That's So, those are exclusive uses.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: under the explu-, w- we'll get to there in a second, but there, it's under the...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Under the exclusio unius principle, D and E are the only purposes that are authorized here, and others are intended to be excluded.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: I mean, as far as I read D and E, they address a a typical, but not most important, regulatory problem.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: I think there's several answers to that question.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: We're We're off on the on the other...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: to the other issue, but let me just answer the question quickly, if I could.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: you don't if if if you characterize B the right way.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: You'd have to, in order to come to that reading, you'd have to separate this this very detailed set of rate formulas from their general jurisdiction to regulate.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: for anything other than cable and wire service.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: whereas for those areas that are within its special expertise, cable and...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: I'm glad you agree with that. I just wonder what's...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: so odd about Congress giving regulatory authority...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: regulatory agency and then taking out two areas, that it happens to be, that the industry is particularly interested in and negotiating a specific formula, while leaving others, times I've seen it a thousand times in regulatory statutes, leaving others for the agency to proceed under normal, ordinary, just, and reasonable rate setting authority.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: I I think another answer, and again, I do want to return to the wireless issue because it does tend to get lost in the shuffle here.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: The Off- White House's Office of Telecommunications Policy wrote two letters to Congress expressing that concern when the act was con- being considered, and Congress said, in the legislative history, quite clearly, we're giving the FCC a very narrow grant of jurisdiction.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: very narrow because they're only talking about attachments to utility poles.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: It's a very narrow aspect of the utilities' overall operations.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Not only are they talking about attachments to utility poles, but they're only talking about attachments to utility poles for two kinds of services.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: two kinds of rates, these are the formulas for those two.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: If you, well, certainly if you read this canon, if you read the statute with the canon of exp- of expressio unius, you there needs to...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Well, a- again, i- th- you would...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: not only fixes rates, it requires access.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: w- what you mean by associated equipment, I suppose, is I don't know, joint boxes, lightening arresters, things of that sort.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Associated equipment is equipment that is used to make the wires and the cables work.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Why couldn't associated equipment mean for the for a wireless company, I guess very few of these wireless companies are entirely wireless.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: So, why couldn't the wireless boxes that they nail on these on these poles as pole attachment be deemed associated equipment of these wires that they use elsewhere?

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Well, again, it's wires, cables, and associated equipment, and not...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: and asso- and associated wires and cables.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: to be associated with the very wires and cables that...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: ejusdem generis principles, in that phrase, wires, cables, and associated equipment, the associated equipment is subordinate to the words that precede it and is delimited by those two words.

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Can you just tell me, where does the term, associated equipment, what section was that in?

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: It's in section two two four D two on page two oh eight A of the...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: when given given the fact, that as somebody pointed out, I guess Justice Scalia pointed out earlier, the wireless companies always have some wires.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Right. It's got to be a...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: I I And I think also one last quick point on on wireless.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: sites that are used by a utility.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: sites that are owned by utility companies.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: to mandatory carriage of wire- of of of of wireless?

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: the ar- that's the government's argument.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: This, under this FCC's und- under the FCC's reading of this statute, which is i- impermissible u- if you read the whole statute, they have they have decided to take this revolutionary expansion of their jurisdiction to regulate this tiny piece of the wireless siting market.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: could I ask you a question that gets you back to the other, to the other issue? I- Is it i- is the government correct that you lose if the, if even if the rate applied by the FCC is wrong, that the only thing you're asking us is to say that the FCC has no jurisdiction? It wouldn't even matter if the FCC should have been treating this under E as telecommunications.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: they didn't say it would be treated under D.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: we're going to use the same rate that Congress specifies in in D.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: But but that's indeed what they wh- what they've done.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: phone company is exempted from the statute.

turns.name: ANTHONY KENNEDY
turns.text: Is is that because they have their own poles?

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: They have very few of their own poles.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: Well I thought the purpose of this and I'm really asking this question because I want to hear what your argument is on the main point in this case, if it's different from what you've already said.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Right, and I I you know understand that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, let's talk about the jurisdictional language...

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: narrowing is language that picks out two categories of rate setting.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: The jurisdictional language is not the as broad as it may appear to be.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: toasters and it doesn't include billboards, I agree.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Yes, but it also doesn't include comingled cable and Internet service.

turns.name: RUTH GINSBURG
turns.text: Well, there's an awkwardness that I don't understand.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Well, Congress has talked sort of out of both sides of its u- mouth about the Internet.

turns.name: RUTH GINSBURG
turns.text: In the in that That's how you read...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: But these cable companies are are not mom and pop shops anymore.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: biggest companies in the United States.

turns.name: RUTH GINSBURG
turns.text: in the legislative history, anything that indicates that Congress wanted to take this benefit away from the cable systems if they were also offering Internet access?

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: L- l- let me i- the answer is yes, and it comes in the definition of a cable system.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Well, I'm, what I'm arguing here...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: if the if it gets into the Internet business, it gets put on the same footing as if you're a local phone company...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Whatever whatever the, utility wants to charge. Right.

turns.name: RUTH GINSBURG
turns.text: And that's the price of getting into the Internet for a cable system.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: It gives up it gives up rate...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: Is its main competitor the local phone...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: there are two big competitors in this business, the local phone company which is unregulated, and the cable company which is seeking this...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: how many poles do the local phone companies own own in relation to the the utilities that you're representing?

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: About eighty or eighty-five percent of poles are owned by the utility companies, fifteen, twenty percent are owned...

turns.name: DAVID SOUTER
turns.text: effect of what Justice Ginsburg is talking about is going to be significantly different in relation to the utilities from what it is in relation to local phone companies.

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: Indeed, but the the re- I think the relevant comparison of that would be how many the phone company, i- it's putting it's putting the...

turns.name: THOMAS P. STEINDLER
turns.text: phone company on, more or less, the same...

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: you're you're you're the ones holding holding the market position.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: the the the the phone companies whatever you want for their use of your...

turns.name: JOHN PAUL STEVENS
turns.text: Mr. Steindler, is am I wrong in thinking May I just ask one question? Am I wrong in thinking that a local phone company can be a utility within the meaning of the statute?

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: Indeed, that I'm wrong, or indeed, it is?

turns.name: WILLIAM REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Feldman, you have two minutes remaining.

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: I think it might be worthwhile to point out that the F the FCC's construction of the act is entitled to deference and is at least a reasonable construction of the act.

turns.name: STEPHEN BREYER
turns.text: quite, I mean, in fairness to what th- h- he was arguing, I mean, a person, were this statute not to apply, could still go either to the local telephone company regulators, which I imagine they would do in the case of the local phone company, could go to the local public utilities commission, or could go to the Federal Power Commission and ask them for the same kind of protection that the FCC might give them if this act does apply.

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Well, I'm I guess, I'm not awa-

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: I'm not aware of what the basis of the Federal Power Commission's...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Right, but what Cong- what what Congress...

turns.name: UNKNOWN JUSTICE
turns.text: I suggest, that I just, I'm not tot- -- I'm not...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: sure about what the l- what the authority of local utility...

turns.name: JAMES A. FELDMAN
turns.text: is over the poles as opposed to the rates...

turns.name: UNKNOWN SPEAKER
turns.text: Could they not I  I'm imagining I just heard about...

turns.name: THE MARSHAL
turns.text: The honorable court is now adjourned until tomorrow at ten o'clock.
